type: SURVEY
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
note: Adding fallback_searchpath param to FileSystemLoader.__init__, get_source(), list_templates() in src/jinja2/loaders.py

fields.note: Adding fallback_searchpath param to FileSystemLoader.__init__, get_source(), list_templates() in src/jinja2/loaders.py

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/jinja2/loaders.py
spec: Add fallback_searchpath support to FileSystemLoader in src/jinja2/loaders.py:

1. FileSystemLoader.__init__ signature gets a new optional keyword param inserted after searchpath (before encoding, or as any additional param - order relative to your own new params doesn't matter as long as it's optional with default None): `fallback_searchpath: t.Optional[t.Union[str, os.PathLike, t.Sequence[t.Union[str, os.PathLike]]]] = None`. Process it exactly like searchpath is processed (wrap single str/PathLike into a list via the same 'not isinstance(x, abc.Iterable) or isinstance(x, str)' check, then os.fspath each item). Store as self.fallback_searchpath: t.List[str] (empty list if None was passed).

2. FileSystemLoader.get_source(): Currently loops over self.searchpath and returns on first match, else raises TemplateNotFound at the end. Change so that after the loop over self.searchpath finds nothing, it then loops over self.fallback_searchpath using the identical lookup logic (same os.path.join(searchpath, *pieces), open_if_exists, decode, mtime/uptodate closure) before raising TemplateNotFound. Simplest implementation: iterate over itertools.chain(self.searchpath, self.fallback_searchpath) (or just concatenate the two lists) in a single loop instead of duplicating the loop body. Primary searchpath entries must always be tried before any fallback_searchpath entry (preserves override priority).

3. FileSystemLoader.list_templates(): Currently loops over self.searchpath building a 'found' set. Extend so it also walks self.fallback_searchpath directories the same way (os.walk with self.followlinks), adding to the same 'found' set (the existing 'if template not in found' dedup already prevents duplicates - so primary-searchpath templates naturally take precedence in dedup terms, though for a plain set the value itself is just the name so this satisfies the 'no duplicate names in output' requirement). Iterate primary searchpath entries before fallback entries. Return sorted(found) as before.

4. Backward compatible: when fallback_searchpath is None/omitted, self.fallback_searchpath == [] and behavior is byte-for-byte identical to current code.

5. Please also update the class docstring to mention the new fallback_searchpath param (optional, one-line addition is fine).

Let me know once implemented (DONE) so I can verify get_source/list_templates behavior against my test scenarios (fallback-only template found, primary overrides fallback duplicate, multiple fallback dirs searched in order).
